title: Mac & Cheese (No Cruelty)
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://www.noracooks.com/vegan-mac-and-cheese/ ✓ VERIFIED
- **Description:** Cashew-and-potato cream cheese sauce tossed with elbow pasta. Comfort-food workhorse — sauce holds well in a bain-marie and reheats into noodles to order.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $1.85 / serving · **Menu price:** $11–14 (entrée) / $7–9 (side)
- **Weight:** 320 g · **Calories:** 540 · **Protein:** 18 g · **Time:** 30 min · **Servings:** 4
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — sauce bulk-preps; just plate over hot pasta.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Cashew → sunflower-seed base | Nut-free | −$0.20 |
| Add Beyond Beef crumble (taco mac) | Hearty entrée | +$1.40 |
| **Premium faux-meat:** Impossible Beef "chili-mac" | Chain-quality cross-utilization | +$1.40 |
description: Cashew-and-potato cream cheese sauce tossed with elbow pasta. Comfort-food workhorse — sauce holds well in a bain-marie and reheats into noodles to order.
difficultyNote: sauce bulk-preps; just plate over hot pasta.
ingredients: 2 cups water (or less if not using vegan cheese, see Notes)
1 (7-oz) bag shredded vegan cheddar cheese, optional
steps: Preheat oven and prep - If planning on baking it with the breadcrumb topping, preheat the oven to 350 degrees F and lightly grease a casserole dish (I used a 9x13 inch dish). Skip this step for stovetop mac and cheese.

Soak the cashews - Soften your cashews by covering them in boiling water for 5 minutes. I do this by heating up water in my tea kettle, and then pouring the boiling water over the cashews in a large glass measuring cup.
Cook pasta - Cook the pasta according to package instructions, but do not overcook. Drain and set aside.

Blend cheese sauce - Drain the soaked cashews and discard the soaking water. Add the cashews, fresh water, lemon juice, nutritional yeast, turmeric, garlic powder, salt and bag of shredded cheese (if using) to a high powered blender and blend until very smooth.
Simply return the pasta to the pot and pour in the cheese sauce. Stir until the sauce thickens and serve immediately.
Make the breadcrumb topping by mixing the breadcrumbs, melted vegan butter and smoked paprika in a small bowl until crumbly and moist.

Add the drained pasta to the prepared casserole dish, and pour in the cheese sauce. Stir to coat the noodles. Sprinkle the breadcrumb mixture on the pasta and bake, uncovered for 15 minutes. I also broiled it for a few minutes until golden brown. Serve immediately and enjoy!

title: Impossible Meatloaf
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://www.noracooks.com/vegan-meatloaf/ ✓ VERIFIED *(Nora Cooks's chickpea-based loaf is the verified base. Recommended: swap in Impossible Beef or Beyond Beef for tighter slice integrity; calibrate binder + cook time during a service test since plant-meat renders fat differently than chickpeas.)*
- **Description:** Built directly on **Impossible Beef** for tight, sliceable texture; ketchup-glazed loaf, par-baked in batches and finished à la minute.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $3.65 / serving · **Menu price:** $18–22
- **Weight:** 285 g · **Calories:** 510 · **Protein:** 28 g · **Time:** 65 min · **Servings:** 6
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — bake whole loaves, slice, finish with glaze under salamander.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| **Lead protein:** Impossible Beef (default) | Best slice integrity | baseline |
| Beyond Beef | Slightly looser; saucier glaze recommended | −$0.05 |
| Lentil-walnut | Budget alt | −$1.80 |
urlNote: Nora Cooks's chickpea-based loaf is the verified base. Recommended: swap in Impossible Beef or Beyond Beef for tighter slice integrity; calibrate binder + cook time during a service test since plant-meat renders fat differently than chickpeas. · URL replaced via url_overrides.json
description: Built directly on **Impossible Beef** for tight, sliceable texture; ketchup-glazed loaf, par-baked in batches and finished à la minute.
difficultyNote: bake whole loaves, slice, finish with glaze under salamander.

alternatives.note: Cheaper, lentil-and-chickpea-based alternative — drop the Impossible Beef and use the same ketchup-glaze finish.

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or a silicone baking mat.
In a bowl, mix all ingredients together (Impossible burger, breadcrumbs, ketchup, garlic powder, Worcestershire sauce, onion powder, parsley, salt, and pepper.
Form into a loaf shape on the baking sheet.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until browned.
Brush some ketchup or BBQ on top if desired, or serve with gravy.

title: BBQ Pulled Jackfruit Sandwiches
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://www.noracooks.com/bbq-jackfruit/ ✓ VERIFIED
- **Description:** Brined young jackfruit shredded and simmered in BBQ sauce. Sandwich on brioche with slaw.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $2.65 / serving · **Menu price:** $12–15
- **Weight:** 280 g · **Calories:** 520 · **Protein:** 11 g · **Time:** 40 min · **Servings:** 4
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — pulled holds in steam table.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| **Premium faux-meat:** Chunk Pulled in BBQ sauce | Heartier, more "meaty" pull, higher protein | +$2.20/serving |
| Beyond Beef "shredded" hack | Looser texture | +$1.00 |
description: Brined young jackfruit shredded and simmered in BBQ sauce. Sandwich on brioche with slaw.

subs.from: **Premium faux-meat:** Chunk Pulled in BBQ sauce

ingredients: 2 20 oz cans young green unripe jackfruit
1/4 tsp salt (or more, depending on taste)
3/4 cup bbq sauce (, plus more for dressing)
3 cups slaw mix ((I use shredded cabbage, carrot, brussel sprouts, baby kale mix))
1 tbsp apple cider vinegar (or lemon juice)
1/2 tbsp maple syrup (or sugar to taste)
dashes of salt and freshly ground black pepper
steps: Drain the jackfruit. Place in a thick kitchen towel, fold and squeeze out as much liquid as possible. This will shred some of the jackfruit. Slice the rest using a knife or separate using hands until somewhat shredded. Remove any hard seeds if any (There are rarely hard seeds in the canned version. The tender seedbox and stem area are all edible).
Add the jackfruit to a large bowl with sliced onion, garlic and ginger. Add bbq seasoning and sugar mixture, and salt and mix well.

Add 1/2 to 2/3 cup bbq sauce and mix. Add 2 tbsp veggie broth if the bbq sauce is too thick and mix in (I use 2 tbsp of broth to clean out the mixing bowl after transferring to the baking dish). Spread the mixture in a large baking dish (parchment lined).

Bake at 400 deg F For 30 minutes. Shred some of the larger pieces by pressing with a spatula. Taste and adjust salt, sweet, heat at this point. Add some salt, sugar/maple syrup and cayenne for heat if needed and mix in and move the jackfruit around.
Spread well again and continue to bake for another 15 to 25 mins (depends on moisture content, pan/oven and preferred texture.) I like some edges caramelized and crispy)
Prepare your buns tacos and slaw. Mix everything under slaw. Taste and adjust flavor. Chill for 15 mins and use.
Mix in 2 to 4 tbsp bbq sauce into the baked jackfruit and assemble the sandwiches with generous layers of jackfruit, more bbq sauce, slaw. You can also fill this in wraps or tacos.

title: Buffalo Cauliflower Wings
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://www.noracooks.com/buffalo-cauliflower-wings/ ✓ VERIFIED
- **Description:** Battered cauliflower florets fried/baked, tossed in vegan buffalo sauce. Bar staple.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $2.10 / serving · **Menu price:** $10–13 (app)
- **Weight:** 220 g · **Calories:** 380 · **Protein:** 8 g · **Time:** 35 min · **Servings:** 4
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Medium** — requires per-plate fry; battered florets need timing.
- **Tags:** ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| **Premium faux-meat:** Beyond Chicken Tenders / Impossible Nuggets tossed buffalo | "Wing"-format, more protein | +$2.40 |
description: Battered cauliflower florets fried/baked, tossed in vegan buffalo sauce. Bar staple.
difficultyNote: requires per-plate fry; battered florets need timing.

subs.from: **Premium faux-meat:** Beyond Chicken Tenders / Impossible Nuggets tossed buffalo

ingredients: 1 tablespoon pure maple syrup or agave
2 tablespoons unsweetened soy milk, or even water, to thin as needed
steps: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees F. Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper. Spray generously with oil.
Wash and cut cauliflower into bite sized pieces.
In a large bowl, add water, flour, garlic and onion powder, and salt. Whisk until well combined.

Add the cauliflower to the batter in the bowl, and toss to combine and really coat the cauliflower. Remove the pieces of cauliflower, one by one, and tap off the excess batter a few times on the side of the bowl.
Place on the prepared baking sheet and make sure they are not touching each other, or they will all get stuck together as they bake. Spray the tops with oil, this will help them get super crispy.
Bake for 20 minutes. Flip them over and bake for 15 more minutes, until golden and crispy.

While the cauliflower are baking, prepare the hot sauce/butter mixture. Melt the butter in a saucepan on the stove over medium heat. Once melted, add the hot sauce, maple syrup or agave and stir. Set aside.

Once the cauliflower have cooked and are crispy, toss the florets with the sauce in a large bowl, then return to the pan. You can serve them immediately, or to make them crispier, put them back in the oven for 15-20 minutes. If you want them super crispy, leave the buffalo sauce on the side for dipping as you go.
Serve with easy vegan ranch (recipe included), my cashew based vegan ranch or vegan blue cheese.
Add all the ranch ingredients to a medium bowl and whisk until combined. Taste and add salt, if desired, or more herbs. That's it! Enjoy.

title: Biscuits & Gravy
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://www.noracooks.com/vegan-biscuits-and-gravy/ ✓ VERIFIED
- **Description:** Flaky vegan biscuits + sausage-pepper-cream gravy. Brunch hero; gravy holds in a bain.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $2.25 / serving · **Menu price:** $11–14 (brunch entrée)
- **Weight:** 320 g · **Calories:** 620 · **Protein:** 16 g · **Time:** 50 min · **Servings:** 6
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — biscuits par-baked, gravy held warm.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| **Premium faux-meat:** Impossible Sausage in gravy | Brand-recognizable; tighter crumble | +$1.40 |
| Beyond Sausage | Slightly fattier mouthfeel | +$1.30 |
description: Flaky vegan biscuits + sausage-pepper-cream gravy. Brunch hero; gravy holds in a bain.
ingredients: 14 ounces vegan sausage crumbles, or chopped pieces (*I used Impossible brand, see Notes)
1 teaspoon salt, or more to taste
steps: In a large pan over medium-high heat, cook the vegan sausage.
Now add the flour, cashew milk and vegan butter. Cook on medium heat for 5-10 minutes, stirring constantly, until the gravy thickens.
Add the thyme, rosemary, red pepper flakes, salt and pepper. Taste the gravy and add any additional seasonings, salt or pepper, as desired.
Serve immediately over warm biscuits and enjoy!

title: Beef Wellington (No Cruelty)
rawTitle: ### Vegan Beef Wellington (**SHOWSTOPPER** — Juicy Marbles Loin)
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://juicymarbles.com/blogs/recipes/vegan-wellington-mushroom-duxelle ✓ VERIFIED *(Juicy Marbles' own official Wellington recipe — uses their whole-cut loin as our spec calls for, includes mushroom duxelles, eggplant layer, blueberry-wine chutney)*
- **Description:** **Lead protein:** Juicy Marbles Whole-Cut Loin (6 oz portion, seared hard) wrapped in mushroom-shallot-thyme duxelles, vegan prosciutto-style crepe layer, and all-butter vegan puff pastry. Egg-washed with maple-soy, scored, and roasted to a deep mahogany. Sliced tableside or à la minute, served with red-wine demi, pommes purée, and roasted hen-of-the-woods. The American steakhouse's plant-based holiday/anniversary plate.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $13.50 / serving · **Menu price:** **$45–52** (upscale ÷ 0.27)
- **Weight:** 380 g · **Calories:** 720 · **Protein:** 38 g · **Time:** 90 min (incl. par-sear, chill, wrap, bake) · **Servings:** 4 (one whole loin yields four 6-oz portions)
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Hard** — multi-component build; pastry must be cold when going in, internal must hit 130°F, slice-cleanly requires a 10-min rest. Par-build whole Wellingtons during prep, finish-bake per ticket window.
- **Tags:** 🥘 (par-build only) · — (per-ticket finish)

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| **Lead protein:** Juicy Marbles Whole-Cut Loin (default) | Closest to traditional Wellington presentation | baseline |
| Juicy Marbles Thick-Cut Filets × 2 stacked | Easier portioning | −$2.00 |
| Chunk Foods Steakhouse Cut 6 oz | Steakhouse-grade, more savings | −$5.50 |
| Mushroom-lentil "loaf" core | Budget-tier Wellington (no faux-meat brand) | −$9.00 → menu $24–29 |
urlNote: Juicy Marbles' own official Wellington recipe — uses their whole-cut loin as our spec calls for, includes mushroom duxelles, eggplant layer, blueberry-wine chutney
description: **Lead protein:** Juicy Marbles Whole-Cut Loin (6 oz portion, seared hard) wrapped in mushroom-shallot-thyme duxelles, vegan prosciutto-style crepe layer, and all-butter vegan puff pastry. Egg-washed with maple-soy, scored, and roasted to a deep mahogany. Sliced tableside or à la minute, served with red-wine demi, pommes purée, and roasted hen-of-the-woods. The American steakhouse's plant-based holiday/anniversary plate.
difficultyNote: multi-component build; pastry must be cold when going in, internal must hit 130°F, slice-cleanly requires a 10-min rest. Par-build whole Wellingtons during prep, finish-bake per ticket window.
ingredients: 1 lb (450 g) seitan, shaped into a log
8 oz (225 g) cremini mushrooms, finely chopped (duxelles)
1 sheet (14 oz) vegan puff pastry, thawed
salt and black pepper to taste

allergenSubs.to: king trumpet mushrooms, scored and seared

title: New York Cheesecake (No Cruelty)
rawBody:
- **URL:** https://www.noracooks.com/vegan-cheesecake/ ✓ VERIFIED *(Nora Cooks — uses Tofutti or Kite Hill cream cheese + coconut cream baked NY-style)*
- **Description:** Cashew-and-tofu-based cream cheese filling on a vegan graham crust, baked in a water bath for the dense, crackless NY style. Holds 4–5 days; portion-cuts cleanly cold. Top per-ticket with macerated berries, salted caramel, or cocoa.
- **Wholesale food cost:** $1.95 / serving · **Menu price:** $9–12
- **Weight:** 220 g · **Calories:** 480 · **Protein:** 9 g · **Time:** 60 min active + 4 hr chill · **Servings:** 12 / 9-inch springform
- **Restaurant difficulty:** **Easy** — bake whole, slice, plate cold per ticket with one warm topping option.
- **Tags:** 🥘 Bulk-Prep · ⚡ Fast-Service

| Sub | Effect | Cost delta |
|---|---|---|
| Cashew-tofu base (default) | Densest NY-style texture | baseline |
| Vegan cream cheese (Kite Hill / Tofutti) base | Faster build, slightly looser | +$0.40 |
| Add seasonal berry compote topping | Premium menu listing | +$0.30 → menu $11–14 |
urlNote: Nora Cooks — uses Tofutti or Kite Hill cream cheese + coconut cream baked NY-style
description: Cashew-and-tofu-based cream cheese filling on a vegan graham crust, baked in a water bath for the dense, crackless NY style. Holds 4–5 days; portion-cuts cleanly cold. Top per-ticket with macerated berries, salted caramel, or cocoa.
difficultyNote: bake whole, slice, plate cold per ticket with one warm topping option.

subs.from: Vegan cream cheese (Kite Hill / Tofutti) base

ingredients: 5 tablespoons melted vegan butter (or coconut oil)
32 ounces (4 8-ounce packages) Tofutti vegan cream cheese (See notes, do NOT use Trader Joe's!)
3 tablespoons lemon juice, from about 2 small lemons
1 1/2 cups fresh strawberries, hulled and sliced
1 teaspoon lemon zest, from 1 small lemon
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F. Wrap a 9 inch springform pan with 1-2 layers of aluminum foil, covering the bottom and the sides. Cut a circle of parchment paper for the bottom of the pan, and spray lightly with oil.

In a medium bowl, add the cookie crumbs, melted vegan butter and sugar. Stir well to combine, then press down into the bottom of the prepared pan. Press down firmly and evenly, going up the sides a little. Set aside.
In the bowl of an electric mixer with the whisk attachment, or using a handheld mixer, beat the vegan cream cheese until smooth, about 1 minute.
Now add the rest of the filling ingredients and beat until smooth, scraping down the sides and bottom of the bowl as needed.
Once it's completely smooth, pour into prepared pan over the crust and spread evenly.

Place in the oven and bake for 50 minutes. Do not open the oven door during this time. Turn off the heat, and let it sit in the oven for 10 more minutes. The cheesecake will be slightly jiggly, and not look very done in the middle. That is correct, it will firm up when it cools.

Remove from the oven, and let it cool for about 15 minutes at room temperature before moving to the refrigerator to cool for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight, before slicing and serving. When ready to serve, slice and serve with any optional toppings.

In a food processor, add 1 1/4 cups of the sliced strawberries (reserving 1/4 cup), sugar and lemon zest. Process until smooth. Toss with the sliced strawberries in a small bowl and chill for at least an hour before serving.
Storing: Leftover cheesecake will keep for up to 7 days in the refrigerator. It can also be frozen.

allergenSubs.to: refined avocado oil or vegan butter
